elements.sent-2411-001: This paper is concerned with effective approximations and wall laws of viscous laminar flows in 3D pipes with randomly rough boundaries.
elements.sent-2411-002: The random roughness is characterized by the boundary oscillation scale $\varepsilon \ll 1 $ and a probability space with ergodicity quantified by functional inequalities.
elements.sent-2411-003: The results in this paper generalize the previous work for 2D channel flows with random Lipschitz boundaries to 3D pipe flows with random boundaries of John type.
elements.sent-2411-004: Moreover, we establish the optimal convergence rates and substantially improve the stochastic integrability obtained in the previous studies.
elements.sent-2411-005: Additionally, we prove a refined version of the Poiseuille's law in 3D pipes with random boundaries, which seems unaddressed in the literature.
elements.sent-2411-006: Our systematic approach combines several classical and recent ideas (particularly from homogenization theory), including the Saint-Venant's principle for pipe flows, the large-scale regularity theory over rough boundaries, and applications of functional inequalities.. {\bf Keywords.}
elements.sent-2411-007: Stationary Navier-Stokes system, Navier's wall laws, randomly rough pipes, functional inequalities, large-scale regularity.
elements.sent-2411-008: This paper is devoted to the effective approximation of viscous flows, governed by the stationary Navier-Stokes system, in 3D pipes with randomly rough boundaries.
elements.sent-2411-009: Understanding the effect on fluid induced by boundary roughness remains a core challenge in fluid dynamics even in the laminar regime when the Reynolds number of the flow is sufficiently small.
elements.sent-2411-010: Indeed, laminar flows with rough boundaries discussed in this paper naturally appear in biomechanics (e.g., blood flow in vessels) and engineering (e.g., corroded or corrugated pipes).
elements.sent-2411-011: If the roughness is relatively mild, one may address such problems by finding analytic solutions \cite{CS72,SJZ01,Wang06,MeiJin2016 or by numerical computations \cite{FN77,DS79,NOK84,MohFlo2013,LLS2019 , albeit not exhaustively.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: These direct approaches are unavailable or require extensive computational resources if the boundaries are more complicated and wildly oscillating at small scales.
elements.sent-2411-013: In the presence of such irregularities, it becomes impractical to record the fluid dynamics meticulously; instead, one should focus on understanding the average fluid configuration.
elements.sent-2411-014: Wall laws or effective boundary conditions serve this purpose.
elements.sent-2411-015: Here we quote a definition from the pioneering work by J\"{a ger-Mikeli\'{c \cite{JM01 : ``In general, the approach of replacing the no-slip condition at rough boundaries with the non-penetration condition plus a relation between the tangential velocity and the shear stress, is called the wall laws." Namely, the rough boundary is replaced by an artificially smoothed one, and an effective boundary condition is imposed to represent the averaged effect of the roughness.}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: Typically, the effective boundary condition for laminar flows is known to be Navier-type slip, and thus called Navier's wall law.
elements.sent-2411-017: As a representative work from the engineering side, Nikuradse \cite{Ni50 empirically studied the wall laws by detailed experiments for turbulent flows; see \cite{KSCT2005,FlaSch2014,CHSF2021 for recent progress in this direction.}}

elements.sent-2411-025: In summary, the previous mathematical study of wall laws mainly targets laminar flows in 2D channels or 3D slabs, which are less applicable compared to those in 3D pipes.
elements.sent-2411-026: Additionally, all of these studies assume that the boundaries are at least locally Lipschitz.
elements.sent-2411-027: The objective of this paper is to develop a systematic approach to study wall laws and quantitative convergence rates for laminar flows in 3D pipes with random roughness.
elements.sent-2411-028: We will also relax the boundary regularity from local Lipschitz boundaries to more generalized boundaries of John type, which are not necessarily local graphs of functions and allow for fractals and cusps.
elements.sent-2411-029: Moreover, we will substantially improve the stochastic integrability under certain ergodicity conditions.
